message:
fix(policy): detect dangerous commands invoked via absolute/relative paths

The is_command_candidate() function only matched bare command names like
"rm", allowing bypass via "/bin/rm -rf /" or "./rm -rf /".

Add path-aware matching: when a token contains '/' or '\\', extract the
basename and check it against COMMAND_TOKENS. This catches /bin/rm,
/usr/bin/curl, ./python3, and similar path-qualified invocations. Also
handles "sudo /bin/rm" via the same path extraction logic.

Rewrite policy_bypass.rs tests to focus on this specific bypass vector
with a repro test covering plain, absolute, and relative path command
detection scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
